title: Warhammer Quest - Let’s Play - Part 7
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TXAe5nqafRY

Daniel (@dungeondive) picks up after the spider lair nightmare. The warriors are battered—Saul has four hit points, Harlan is topped up—but the minotaur still stands. Daniel addresses the two-player scaling issue head-on, reducing the minotaur to half wounds and one attack based on a viewer's suggestion to scale gold values proportionally.

The fight is tense. Harlan goes berserk and chains attacks but can't quite land the killing blow on the minotaur. Saul breaks free from his webbing and unleashes the Amulet of Holy Fire, blasting the beast down to two hit points. A failed Lifebringer spell wastes the healing scroll on double dice. Harlan finally drops the minotaur and carves through the remaining orc with a deathblow chain. Both warriors survive with Saul at four wounds and no healing items left. They collect three treasures—a Talon of Death, a healing salve, and an Ensorcelled Blade that grants +1 to hit rolls. Then comes the bridge. Daniel rolls for each warrior crossing the bottomless pit. Harlan passes. Saul—the warrior who died on this very bridge in the first dungeon—rolls a two. Safe. The episode ends with three more rooms to explore before the objective, and genuine optimism for the first time in the campaign.

----

How do you handle boss monster scaling when playing Warhammer Quest with fewer than four warriors? House rules or strict rules-as-written?
